title: The KV-Cache, Made Concrete
tagline: Why LLM inference is memory-bound — sized in megabytes per token.
summary: Why does a 70B LLM need an 80GB GPU even at batch size 1? Not the weights — the KV-cache. Slide model size, context length, batch size, and head sharing (GQA) and watch the cache size dominate. Then toggle PagedAttention to see how vLLM gets back capacity.
highlights: Sliders for params, layers, context, batch — total GB of KV-cache shown live.
Toggle MHA vs GQA(8) vs MQA — see the memory cliff for each.
PagedAttention demo: contiguous vs paged allocation, fragmentation reclaim.
